title: Trigonometry
blurb: Unit-by-unit practice for the Trigonometry course — right triangles to identities and the laws.

units.title: Right-Triangle Trigonometry
units.blurb: SOH-CAH-TOA: name the sides, pick the ratio, solve for any missing side or angle — then aim it at towers, ramps, and shadows.

units.title: Special Triangles & Exact Values
units.blurb: Half a square and half an equilateral: the two triangles behind every exact value of 30°, 45°, and 60°.

units.title: Radians & the Unit Circle
units.blurb: Angles measured by arc, a circle where cosine and sine ARE the coordinates — and exact values in every quadrant.

units.title: Graphs of Trig Functions
units.blurb: The circle unrolled into a wave: amplitude, period, phase, midline — and tangent's wilder portrait.

units.title: Identities & Equations
units.blurb: The Pythagorean identity, sum and double-angle formulas — and solving equations whose unknown is an angle.

units.title: Laws of Sines & Cosines
units.blurb: Trigonometry for EVERY triangle: the sine area formula, both laws, and the strategy for solving any triangle from any three facts.

variants.statement: A right triangle has hypotenuse $4$ and an acute angle of $30°$. Find the side OPPOSITE the $30°$ angle.
variants.explanation: The opposite side over the hypotenuse is the sine: side $= 4\sin 30° = 2$.

variants.statement: A right triangle has hypotenuse $4$ and an acute angle of $30°$. Find the side ADJACENT the $30°$ angle.
variants.explanation: The adjacent side over the hypotenuse is the cosine: side $= 4\cos 30° = 2 \sqrt{3}$.

forms.title: From sin to cos and tan
forms.skill: Build the triangle from the triple; read the other ratios.

variants.statement: An acute angle $\alpha$ has $\sin\alpha = \dfrac{3}{5}$. Find $\cos\alpha$.
variants.explanation: Use the $3\text{-}4\text{-}5$ right triangle (legs $3$, $4$, hypotenuse $5$); read off $\cos\alpha = \frac{4}{5}$.

forms.title: The double-angle formula
forms.skill: sin 2α = 2 sin α cos α — never just double.

variants.statement: An acute angle has $\sin\alpha = \dfrac{3}{5}$ and $\cos\alpha = \dfrac{4}{5}$. Find $\sin 2\alpha$.
variants.explanation: $\sin 2\alpha = 2\sin\alpha\cos\alpha = 2\cdot\frac{3}{5}\cdot\frac{4}{5} = \frac{24}{25}$.

variants.statement: An acute angle has $\sin\alpha = \dfrac{3}{5}$ and $\cos\alpha = \dfrac{4}{5}$. Find $\cos 2\alpha$.
variants.explanation: $\cos 2\alpha = \cos^2\alpha - \sin^2\alpha = \frac{16}{25} - \frac{9}{25} = \frac{7}{25}$.

forms.skill: c² = a² + b² − 2ab·cos C; 60° and 120° keep it exact.

variants.statement: A triangle has sides $2$ and $3$ with a $60°$ angle between them. Find the third side.
variants.explanation: Law of cosines: $c^2 = 2^2 + 3^2 - 2\cdot2\cdot3\cos 60° = 4+9-6 = 7$, so $c = \sqrt{7}$.
